title: Intensely Gooey French Pork Tenderloin Confit
description: A cultured and gooey light French dish featuring pork tenderloin, ferment-kimchi, and nut-free-friendly ingredients in a confit form, originating from the Provence region.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork tenderloin with salt, black pepper, and thyme.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the duck fat over medium heat.
Sear the pork tenderloin until browned on all sides, then remove it from the pot.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook until fragrant.
Add the ferment-kimchi, bay leaves, and browned pork tenderloin to the pot.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the confit for 2 hours, or until the meat is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the confit warm, garnished with fresh thyme and a side of crusty bread.
